ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers green tea broadly; no specific jasmine-infused or packaged no-sugar RTD subcode exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers green tea as a substance; no jasmine-specific or RTD packaged variant subcode confirmed. Closest defensible code used.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (4 mg/100 g) and zero sugar; green tea polyphenols may modestly support endothelial function
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened jasmine green tea with only 4 mg sodium per 100 g is suitable for hypertension management; limit to 2–3 servings daily due to moderate caffeine content, and avoid adding sugar or honey.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; EGCG catechins may modestly improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0 g sugar and 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, this unsweetened jasmine green tea does not raise blood glucose and is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes; confirm no added sweeteners on the ingredient label before consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and low sodium; green tea catechins associated with modest LDL reduction
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unsweetened jasmine green tea is a heart-friendly beverage with no fat, no cholesterol, and minimal sodium; regular moderate consumption (2–3 cups daily) is consistent with cardiovascular health guidelines, though caffeine should be moderated in those with arrhythmia.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EGCG and catechins with documented anti-inflammatory properties (TNF-alpha and IL-6 modulation)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Unsweetened jasmine green tea contains EGCG and catechins with anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; avoid adding sugar or milk which reduce polyphenol bioavailability, and moderate caffeine intake if on hepatotoxic RA medications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jasmine Green Tea (No Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-drink (RTD) beverage made from commercially brewed green tea scented with jasmine flowers, packaged without any added sugar or sweeteners. The product is widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia in 500 ml bottles and cans. The flavour profile is light, floral, and mildly astringent, with the characteristic vegetal notes of green tea complemented by the delicate fragrance of jasmine blossoms. The tea is brewed from Camellia sinensis leaves and scented through traditional jasmine layering or flavour infusion during commercial production. Nutritionally, this product is essentially calorie-free: it contains 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, and 0 g sugar per 100 g, with only 4 mg sodium per 100 g as reported by the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore Foodo database. It is naturally free of allergens and suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free diets. The beverage contains bioactive polyphenols — primarily epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and other catechins — which have been associated in peer-reviewed literature with modest improvements in insulin sensitivity, LDL cholesterol reduction, anti-inflammatory activity (TNF-alpha and IL-6 modulation), and endothelial function support. Clinically, this product is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis, provided no sugar or sweeteners are added after opening. Caffeine content is moderate and should be limited to 2–3 servings per day. The glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with review_status reviewed and confidence 0.95 in the input record. All per-100g values are consistent with the known nutritional profile of unsweetened RTD jasmine green tea. Exact match to an approved primary source.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — this is an unsweetened brewed tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — no fat-bearing ingredients are present in this product formulation. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — no sugar or starch is added; the product is genuinely unsweetened. energy_kcal is naturally zero as all macros are zero. sugar_g is confirmed zero. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this packaged RTD jasmine green tea product is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 as required by template numeric field contract; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey that GI is clinically inapplicable for this zero-carbohydrate beverage (0.0 g carbohydrate per 100g confirmed per HPB). GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no GI value exists for zero-carbohydrate unsweetened tea. Ontology codes are approximate matches — no exact FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT subclass exists for jasmine-scented unsweetened RTD packaged green tea; closest defensible codes used with gap notes. Caffeine content is not quantified in the HPB record; typical RTD jasmine green tea contains approximately 15–30 mg caffeine per 100 ml but this is not confirmed for this specific product.
